name: websec
description: Full-stack web offensive tool: OSINT, headers, CORS, cookies, SQLi (error+time-blind), XSS, CSRF, 403 bypass, open redirect, framework CVEs, WAF fingerprinting, directory discovery, spidering, Google dorks
author: dezthejackal

optional_dependencies.requests: Better HTTP handling, session management - pip3 install requests
optional_dependencies.beautifulsoup4: HTML parsing for spider/form detection - pip3 install beautifulsoup4
optional_dependencies.dnspython: Advanced DNS lookups - pip3 install dnspython

help.description: A terminal-based web security research tool for bug bounty hunters and security researchers. Covers web OSINT, security header auditing, CORS testing, cookie analysis, directory discovery, SQL injection detection, XSS detection, web spidering, and WAF fingerprinting. Every operation includes educational explanations so users understand what they're doing and why it matters. For authorized testing only.

operation.description: The security testing operation to perform
examples: recon - Full OSINT: DNS, WHOIS, SSL certs, robots.txt, tech stack
headers - Security headers audit (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc)
cors - Test for CORS misconfigurations that enable cross-origin attacks
cookies - Audit cookie flags: Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite
dirs - Brute-force common paths: .env, .git, admin panels, backups
sqli - SQL injection detection via error patterns
xss - Reflected XSS detection via input reflection
spider - Crawl the site, discover all URLs, forms, JS files
dork - Generate Google dorks and OSINT resource links for the target
ssl - Deep SSL/TLS analysis: cipher suites, version, cert info
waf - Detect WAF presence and type (Cloudflare, ModSecurity, etc)
vulnlib - Browse built-in vulnerability knowledge base

test_url.description: URL with parameters for injection testing (sqli/xss operations)

max_pages.description: Maximum pages to crawl in spider operation

wordlist_file.description: Path to custom wordlist file for directory discovery

examples.description: Web OSINT - gather everything publicly available (start here)
Discover hidden files and admin panels
SQL injection detection on a parameter
Generate OSINT dorks and resource links
Full assessment - all checks (run with authorization)
Browse the vulnerability knowledge base to learn

features: Web OSINT: DNS records, WHOIS, SSL cert inspection, Wayback Machine, crt.sh
Security headers audit: HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options
CORS misconfiguration testing: wildcard, origin reflection, credential exposure
Cookie security audit: Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite flags
Directory/file discovery: 100+ built-in paths, custom wordlist support
SQL injection detection: error-based, 15+ database error patterns
Reflected XSS detection: HTML encoding bypass detection
Web spider/crawler: URL mapping, form discovery, JS file enumeration
Google dork generation: 18+ dork queries, OSINT resource links
SSL/TLS analysis: version, cipher, cert details, HTTP→HTTPS redirect
WAF detection: Cloudflare, AWS WAF, ModSecurity, Akamai, Imperva, F5, Sucuri
Built-in vulnerability knowledge base: OWASP Top 10 with educational context
Educational [LEARN] context at every step - teaches users as they test
Structured JSON output for integration with other SecV tools
Graceful degradation: works with stdlib only, enhanced with requests/bs4

OWASP_A03_Injection: SQL injection - error-based detection (15+ DB patterns)

installation_tiers.basic: Core functionality with Python stdlib - DNS, WHOIS, SSL, headers, dirs, dork, WAF
installation_tiers.standard: Full functionality with requests - better HTTP, session handling, CORS testing
installation_tiers.full: All features with bs4 - HTML parsing, form detection, spider, XSS/SQLi form testing

notes: ⚠️  AUTHORIZATION REQUIRED: Only test systems you own or have written permission to test
⚠️  Bug bounty scope: always read the program's scope before testing
⚠️  SQLi/XSS tests only use safe, error-detection payloads - no data modification
💡 Start with 'recon' - it's non-intrusive and reveals the most information
💡 Run 'vulnlib' to learn about vulnerabilities before testing for them
💡 Use 'dork' to find OSINT without touching the target server
💡 For SSL testing, also use: https://www.ssllabs.com/ssltest/
💡 For deep testing, pair with Burp Suite Community (free): manual proxy + repeater
💡 Always try manual testing after automated tools - logic flaws need human eyes
💡 Responsible disclosure: follow the program's rules, report clearly, be patient
📚 Every operation prints [LEARN] context explaining what we're doing and why
help.legal_disclaimer: This tool is designed exclusively for authorized security testing, bug bounty research, and educational purposes. Users must obtain explicit written permission before testing any system they do not own. Unauthorized testing may violate the Computer Fraud and Abuse Act (CFAA), Computer Misuse Act, and similar laws globally. The authors assume no liability for misuse. Always follow responsible disclosure practices and bug bounty program rules.

operation.description: Operation: recon, headers, cors, cookies, dirs, sqli, xss, csrf, bypass_403, open_redirect, framework_cves, file_upload, rate_limit, spider, dork, ssl, waf, full

test_url.description: URL with query params for SQLi/XSS testing (e.g. https://example.com/search?q=test)

bypass_path.description: Path to test 403 bypass on (used with bypass_403 operation)

cookies.description: Session cookies for authenticated scanning (key=value; key2=value2)

headers_str.description: Custom request headers (Header-Name: value; Header2: value)

wordlist_file.description: Path to custom wordlist for directory discovery

findings.description: Vulnerability findings with severity, CWE, OWASP mapping, fix, and learning context

finding_count.description: Count of findings by severity: critical, high, medium, low, info
